{"key_event":"French Mandate creates Greater Lebanon","reasoning":"French-backed mandatory institutions created a functioning administration, constitution, and coercive apparatus, but sovereignty and reach were bounded by reliance on French authority and local sectarian notables. Civil society had meaningful communal institutions, schools, press, and petition/protest politics, yet mobilization was fragmented and only partly able to constrain the mandate state.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":5.0,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":5.0}
